title: Work types
description: Canonical taxonomy of commit work types used by release-kit. Drives section ordering, audience classification, and the `!` (breaking) policy. The structured single-source-of-truth for everything that previously lived in `DEFAULT_WORK_TYPES` and `cliff.toml.template` group definitions.

$schema.description: Optional reference to this schema for IDE validation.

tiers.description: Render tiers in canonical order. Each entry's `tier` field must reference one of these names.

types.description: Work-type entries in canonical render order (tier order, then row order within tier).

tier.description: Render tier this entry belongs to. Must match one of the names in the top-level `tiers` array.

key.description: Canonical work-type identifier (e.g. `feat`, `fix`). Must be globally unique across all entries.

aliases.description: Alternate identifiers that resolve to this work type. Must be globally unique and not collide with any `key`.

emoji.description: Decorative emoji rendered as the prefix of the section heading.

label.description: Human-readable section label (rendered as `${emoji} ${label}` in changelogs and release notes).

breakingPolicy.description: Policy governing the `!` (breaking) marker for this type. `forbidden`: `!` is a policy violation. `optional`: `!` is allowed. `required`: bare type is a policy violation; only the `!` form is accepted.

excludedFromChangelog.description: When `true`, this type's commits never appear in any changelog or release note. Currently only `fmt` sets this. Default: `false`.
